claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 7 years old and does not live with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 7 (under 16)
  - Claimant contributes £50.00/week towards child's upkeep (>= £26.05) and no other claimaint has priority
  - Child in local authority care for 12 weeks (>8) and does not spend 24+ hours/week at home
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child in local authority care for 12 weeks (>8) and does not spend 24+ hours/week at home
circumstances: Claimant contributes £50.00/week towards child's upkeep (>= £26.05) and no other claimaint has priority
Child in local authority care for 12 weeks (>8) and does not spend 24+ hours/week at home
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To do this, I'll need to gather some information about you and your child or children.

Let me start with the first question:

**How many children are you inquiring about for Child Benefit?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hi! I'm inquiring about one child - my son Alex. He's 7 years old, but he doesn't live with me at the moment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. I can see you have one son, Alex, who is 7 years old and doesn't currently live with you.

To help me assess your eligibility, I need to understand more about Alex's living situation:

**Where is Alex currently living, and who is he living with?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex is currently in local authority care - he's been living in a children's home for a few months now.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that. It's helpful to know that Alex is in local authority care in a children's home.

Before I can give you a final answer, I need to check the specific rules about children in care. Let me review the relevant guidance.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Good, I've reviewed the guidance on children in care. Now I have an important question:

**Does Alex spend at least 24 hours a week at home with you, or does he spend 2 nights in a row each week (or 7 days or more in a row) at home?**

This is important because there are exceptions to the general rule about children in care.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he doesn't come home regularly like that. He stays at the children's home full-time.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that clarification. I now have all the information I need to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

Based on the rules I've reviewed, let me explain the situation:

According to the guidance on Child Benefit for children in care, you are usually no longer entitled to Child Benefit after a child has been in care for 8 weeks. There are exceptions to this rule, but they require that:
- The child spends at least 24 hours a week at home, OR
- The child spends 2 nights in a row each week (or 7 days or more in a row) at home

Since Alex has been in the children's home full-time without regular contact at home, he doesn't meet these exceptions.

Let me now provide you with my formal assessment:

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Summary:** Unfortunately, you are not currently eligible for Child Benefit for Alex. This is because he has been in local authority care for more than 8 weeks and doesn't meet the exceptions to continue payments (which would require him to spend regular time at home with you).

However, if Alex's circumstances change in the future—for example, if he were to spend 2 nights in a row each week or 7 days or more in a row at home—you may be able to claim Child Benefit again. I'd recommend contacting HMRC to discuss your specific situation and any potential options.

Is there anything else I can help you with?

reasonings: Alex is 7 years old and has been in local authority care (children's home) for a few months. According to the Child Benefit rules for children in care, you are usually no longer entitled to Child Benefit after more than 8 weeks in care. Exceptions apply only if the child spends at least 24 hours a week at home, or 2 nights in a row each week (or 7 days or more in a row) at home. Since Alex stays at the children's home full-time with no regular time spent at home, he does not meet any of the exceptions. Therefore, you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: You are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex because he has been in local authority care (children's home) for more than 8 weeks and does not meet the exceptions that would allow you to continue receiving payments. These exceptions require regular contact at home, which is not happening in this case.">
